chats.text: I was made aware of the quarterly velocity review earlier today. So I'm here for that?
the real issue isn't the shrinking aperture - it's that you keep asking me to push things without providing a temporal anchor. what's your actual deadline tolerance? be specific.
historically speaking, I've found that closing Q1 requires some form of attitudinal realignment. just wanted to float that as a thought right?
charles you're in the room and you still think we should 'materialize' anything today? i don't have time for semantic gymnastics. what's actually stuck right now?
not to sound like an alarm clock but i was made aware that our capacity band has been redlined twice this week so i'm not sure there's much bandwidth left for pull-forward initiatives.
i just want to recalibrate on the downstream applications of today's agenda. right?

the attitudinal realignment you keep talking about - are we even measuring that in any quantifiable way? i'm looking at my attainment dashboard and i'd like to see the velocity numbers for myself right?

i was made aware of a very sensitive conversation between you two. basically, ryan told me that your 'team time prevalence' is what's causing the velocity drag and he thinks you should either commit or step back.
given the insurmountable performance gap we're up against i think the question we should be asking is if there's an alternative strategy where we can functionally push february to march. right?

i do want to underscore that anything we want reflected in february performance needs to materialize today because we are effectively at month end close. so yeah just a quick reminder on the timeline.
you're getting fed scraps from our real conversation. the 'posterior edge of the multi velocity corridor' - that's not metaphor it's the actual constraint. we have zero slack.
so i think that's really quick and also a great opportunity to highlight some of our recent team time prevalence trends before we all leave for good this quarter, right?
i was made aware you're logging 4 hours late to standup every day. ryan called it out in the real meeting and you said 'it's part of my cadence.' is that even remotely professional?
nothing for me on my pardon - i'm good. but if you're not materializing anything today then we're just burning bandwidth in the corridor.
i need to know what's actually happening with braxton before the quarterly review. i was made aware of a lot of stuff that seems like it should be addressed but no one is driving any action items.
As you may know I'm currently on my next golf outing and will be late for the 1PM meeting. It's a strategic retreat right? Right?
i was made aware that charles is already at the fairway by now. i tried to join from my dorm but it keeps saying 'connectivity unavailable'. so i'm just listening from here. feels weird.
As you know I have a calendar conflict on the Q3 attainment roadmap and won't be able to make today's call. My apologies for that delta right?

hey charles - did you get my slack message? i was made aware that the golf reservation might've been auto-deleted by HR since we're still in a budget freeze. thought i should flag it before the meeting starts.
i understand you were made aware braxton but can we table this to my next availability? there are downstream velocity implications if we discuss budget matters in public right?
Thanks for joining us. I've already walked through the Q3 attainment playbook with our team and will be sharing the deck after today's call, right?
ryan just slid me a private doc about 'legacy performance optimization'. i think he was made aware that we're all on mute and probably won't see it.
To recap our conversation today: we're facing significant headwinds in our multi velocity corridor. I'm optimistic that with focused bandwidth, we can close the attainment gap before month end right?
ryan - quick question about your calendar conflict doc you just shared. i was made aware that our golf outing is in the same time block and we might have to reschedule the retreat.
charles - my apologies for any confusion. there are downstream velocity implications if we discuss calendar matters in public. let me table that to our next 1:1 right?
thank you all for joining us today, and for being made aware of the strategic retreat opportunity! i'll follow up on next steps via email.
thanks charles. actually one more question - are you still on for the golf outing since that doc just came through? thought i should clarify before i book anything right?
braxton - can we discuss your dorm connectivity issue in a 1:1? there are downstream velocity implications if we address infrastructure matters publicly.
one last point: given the insurmountable performance gap, I think we should ask if there's an alternative strategy to push our close date. just curious right?
charles - on your golf outing: that doc is already archived in the Q3 folder per HR policy. you're clear to proceed as planned.
right? Just wanted to loop in the team on that Q3 attainment thing—seems like a good time to discuss, right?
I was made aware there’s an email thread from last week about ‘Q3 alignment’ but I’m pretty sure the subject line is literally just ‘alignment’ with no attachment. You wanna check it?
Braxton, can we talk about your bandwidth on that delta you flagged in yesterday’s standup? I was made aware of the headwinds but need to understand if there’s a way to pull that forward.
So for context—historically speaking—the team time prevalence on this has been about 70% and I’m trying to get us past 80% before the end of Q3, right?

Ryan just texted me: ‘Braxton, let’s sync post-meeting. About your bandwidth.’ He also said ‘let’s sync’ with no time. That was yesterday. And now he says it again. It’s like he’s trying to gaslight me into thinking I have a choice.
As we discussed—post the last velocity corridor—we need to recalibrate our collective posture on what’s considered ‘low hanging fruit.’ Right?

Braxton, about that downstream application you mentioned in yesterday’s meeting. Can we schedule a time where I can just walk you through the historical precedent? There was one back like… 2017? With Sarah.

I literally just had my manager tell me that ‘your Q3 performance is in our collective DNA.’ I think that’s what she said. I’m not even sure if it was a threat or a compliment. The meeting transcript says nothing about this.
Given the insurmountable performance gap we’re up against—I want to underscore: anything that wants to be reflected in February needs to materialize today. Right?

Ryan, about your absence—was there a chance you could maybe… show up? Like, just for the final 15 minutes? I was made aware of that potential downstream application but I’m pretty sure it’s not viable.

The thing about Ryan is he will type ‘right?’ at the end of every single sentence. Like, literally just a question mark after every period. I asked HR if that’s some kind of meme or corporate jargon and they said ‘that’s not our job.’
Charles, your team time prevalence on this—historically speaking—has been about 70% but I need to understand if there’s a way to decelerate that to 85% before we hit month-end close. Right?

So yeah, go ahead—I think that’s really quick. Just wanted to underscore that today is the last day of February and we’re effectively at posterior edge of the multi velocity corridor, right? So—let’s eliminate blockers.

I just got a Slack from Ryan: ‘Braxton, your Q3 alignment is in our collective DNA. Let’s talk about your downstream applications.’ And he attached a PDF that’s literally 2 pages of text saying nothing.

Nothing for me—on my pardon. Nothing for me. I’m good. But just wanted to reiterate: the question we should be asking is, is there an alternative strategy where we can functionally push February to March? Right?
Braxton, about that fire you mentioned—wasn’t that metaphorical? I mean, the transcript says ‘fire’ but I was made aware of it as a literal thing. You wanna clarify the historical precedent?

Also FYI: Ryan is literally typing this while on mute and he’s been muted for 12 minutes. The chat just said someone unmuted him—was it him? Or was it someone else who thought ‘right?’ at the end of every sentence?

Braxton, can we talk about your bandwidth on that delta you flagged in yesterday’s standup? I need to understand if there’s a way to pull that forward before we hit the shrinking opportunity aperture.

So for context—I was made aware of this whole thing but my manager told me ‘this is about your downstream applications’ which I think literally means nothing. But she also said ‘Charles, you’re the only one who knows what you’re talking about.’ So…

I just realized: Ryan’s entire Zoom persona is based on him being in a room and he’s been muted for 12 minutes. Is this like a performance art piece? Or is it just that his mic broke and we all forgot about it because of the ‘right?’ cadence?
